issues.title: ChatClient.ChatClientRequestSpec need a function
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, the feature you are about to request might have already been requested.

**Expected Behavior**

 ChatClient.ChatClientRequestSpec need a function like <I, O> ChatClientRequestSpec function(FunctionCallbackWrapper<?,?> wrapper);

**Current Behavior**
not have a function

**Context**
we use lamda function and the function can not get the inputType,so if add the ChatClientRequestSpec function(FunctionCallbackWrapper<?,?> wrapper); we can set the inputType self

comments.body: we need a function like this to register a FunctionCallbackWrapper direct at ChatClient, so we can set the  inputType at FunctionCallbackWrapper

> @xixingya the `FunctionCallbackWrapper` and even the `FunctionCallback` interface are implementation details that we should not be expose those in the chat client API.
> 
> If I understand your use case, you need an abstraction that can pass the input type explicitly?
> 
> Would something like this work for you:
> 
> ```java
> interface FunctionSpec<I> {
>      String getName();
>      String getDescription();
>      Class<I> getInputType; // optional explicit type
>      String getInputTypeSchema(); // optional
> }
> ```
> 
> and ChatCient api like this:
> 
> ```java
> <I2, I, O> ChatClientRequestSpec function(FunctionSpec<I2> functionSpec, java.util.function.Function<I, O> function);
> ```
> 
> This would allow your to pass an explicit input type (I2)

ok. I will change the pr to add function like this.

issues.title: Could not parse multipart form.
issues.body:
![1721054621586](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6d770db5-f63c-4df0-9556-3eedf4037070)


The audio file is put under resources. Here is my code.
`@RestController
public class TranscriptionAPIController {

    @Resource
    private OpenAiAudioTranscriptionModel openAiAudioTranscriptionModel;

    @GetMapping("ai/test6")
    public String test6(ServletRequest request){

    @GetMapping("ai/test7")
    public String test7(ServletRequest request){

        OpenAiAudioTranscriptionOptions options = OpenAiAudioTranscriptionOptions.builder()

            .withModel("whisper-1")
            .withTemperature(0F)
            .withResponseFormat(TranscriptResponseFormat.TEXT)
            .build();

        ClassPathResource resource = new ClassPathResource("tjf.flac");
        String result = openAiAudioTranscriptionModel.call(resource);
        System.out.println(result);
        return result;
    }
}`

Access http://localhost:8080/ai/test7.

The console prints the following content as the picture shows:

comments.body: I did some search and found some people successfully ran the same code and did not got the issue.

![a](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72cad4d4-56ec-4f08-b602-87c13793c019)
After debug, I found that you must add the the code in the image above so that you can run successfully.

But there's a problem, the official website shows it has the default value json, I think it's a bug.
![b](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1a399748-3eac-47ee-b759-5831ebd94b47)

Additionally, if you add
`.withResponseFormat(TranscriptResponseFormat.TEXT)`
you will get the same problem.
@YuJiaoYan1024 I believe this is resolved by changing the response format. Please reopen if that is not the case.

issues.title: Whether multiple APIkeys conflict?
issues.body:
- For https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/ee19ee1db958f02982a29ff13d8fb04d6d415b08/spring-ai-spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/autoconfigure/azure/openai/AzureOpenAiAutoConfiguration.java#L62

This apiKey is currently set globally, so if there are multiple apikeys in an application, will there be conflicts?
Is it put into mode filesl? It can be solved by passing apiKey through request.

comments.body: @tzolov Can you give some suggestions? please
I'd also like to have the possibility to set the apikey at runtime (when actually doing the call)
There is this new PR that should help.  #3037
@yuluo-yx Hope the mutate option addressed via #3037 helps with your use case. Please feel free to re-open the issue if your case requires any specific support from Spring AI. Thanks!
